channel.title: Terms on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in Terms on emsenn.net

item.title: Overrepresentation of Man
item.description: The overrepresentation of Man is Sylvia Wynter’s term for the structural condition in which the modern Western secular subject — Man, in two successive overrepresentations (Man1 of the rational political subject, Man2 of the bio-economic subject) — has been mistaken for the human itself. The mistake is not an error of perception or a correctable bias. It is the structural form of modern knowledge: the conditions under which something counts as rational, scientific, fully human, normatively standard. Other genres of the human are positioned, by the overrepresentation, as the constitutive outside that the figure of Man requires in order to remain Man. To unsettle the overrepresentation is to begin the work of constructing a new descriptive statement of the human — a project Wynter calls “the human, after Man.”
